events.event_description: U.S. Legation established in France when Benjamin Franklin, Minister Plenipotentiary, presented credentials.

evidence.quote: Benjamin Franklin, Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1778-09-14. Credentials presented: 1779-03-23. Superseded 1785-05-17.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation in Paris closed by Chargé d'Affaires ad interim Thomas Pennant Barton, who had been recalled.

evidence.quote: Thomas Pennant Barton, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Legation Paris closed 1835-11-08. Note: Barton closed the legation because he had been recalled.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation in Paris re-opened when Lewis Cass, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, presented credentials.

evidence.quote: Lewis Cass, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1836-10-04. Credentials presented: 1836-12-01. Left post on 1842-11-12.

events.event_description: U.S. mission elevated to Embassy when James Biddle Eustis presented credentials as the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to France.

evidence.quote: James Biddle Eustis, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1893-04-08. Credentials presented: 1893-05-06. Presented recall on 1897-05-24.

events.event_description: France severed diplomatic relations with the United States, ending U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Somerville Pinkney Tuck, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. France severed diplomatic relations with the United States on 1942-11-08.

events.event_description: U.S. Embassy in Paris re-opened to the public with Jefferson Caffery in charge, pending presentation of his letter of credence.

evidence.quote: Note: The Embassy in Paris had been opened to the public December 1, 1944, with Caffery in charge pending presentation of his letter of credence.
